title: Configure the Connection from the Client to the Server
breadcrumbs: Configure the Connection from the Client to the Server
text: Configure the Connection from the Client to the Server To connect to a Uniface Server that provides database or file access, or remote processing, you must specify the path to the Uniface Server in the client application's assignment file. Ensure that the network is operating properly by ‘pinging’ the host server. For example, on Windows select Run from the Microsoft Windows Start menu and enter ping HostID , where HostID is the identifier of the host server. For example: ping ABC012345 Copy or for TCP6: ping -6 ABC012345 Copy If the connection is working properly, you should receive a reply similar to the following: Reply from 172.11.33.130: bytes=32 time=4ms TTL=255 Copy or: ABC012345 is alive Copy In the [PATHS] section of the client assignment file, specify the network connection string and credentials required for each required Uniface Server. For more information, see Path-to-Connector Assignments . For example: [PATHS] $TCP1 = TCP:ABC012345+13001|username|password|Sales Copy $TCP1 is a user-defined path name TCP is the three-letter code for the TCP/IP network connector ABC012345 is the machine where the Uniface Router is running 13001 is the port on which the Uniface Router is listening for client requests username|password is the username and password of the target server Sales is the name of the Uniface Server (UST) as it is known to the Uniface Router Note: For better security, you can use the TLS connector instead of the TCP connector. You can also encrypt the user name and password in your assignment file or open statement using pathscrambler . Related Links TLS Connector The TLS network connector provides an encrypted network connection between the Uniface Router and its Uniface Servers and client applications. It can also be used by the UPOPMAIL component to connect to SMTP servers over TLS. Pathscrambler The Pathscrambler is command-line utility for encrypting sensitive information and values in assignment files and login strings. These may be used in assignment files, servlet settings, the open ProcScript statement and the /log (login) command line switch. Uniface automatically decrypts any data that has been encrypted with the Pathscrambler. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
